public_metrics.rating_note: Search result showed a five-star aggregate at collection time.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers praise advice that feels honest, fair, and not optimized for the largest possible sale.
observations.decision_relevance: Trust-sensitive buyers may respond better to tradeoffs than to one-sided benefit claims.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers repeatedly value being able to reach someone and get timely answers.
observations.decision_relevance: A stalled quote may only need a concrete answer, not a broader marketing sequence.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive reviews link satisfaction to promises being met across the project.
observations.decision_relevance: Pre-contract trust can improve when commitments are specific and later verifiable.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive reviews use practical language about clean, punctual, coordinated installation work.
observations.decision_relevance: Some buyers worry about disruption and craftsmanship more than abstract ROI.

observations.what_customers_say: Some positive review language extends into operation and post-install help.
observations.decision_relevance: Aftercare can be part of the buyer's trust calculation before signing.

cross_source_notes: Fair advice and responsiveness are recurring positive themes across multiple review platforms.
Clean installation language can support risk-sensitive buyer patterns, but only with installer-owned proof.
caveats: The visible page slice is mainly positive.
Use the themes as proof categories, not as claims about any other installer.
